beaver-meadow-cabin.name: Beaver Meadow Cabin
beaver-meadow-cabin.notes: On Monroe Skyline near Mollys Starks Balcony, shown on Monroe Skyline Map at GMCHQ

bourn-pond-shelter-north.name: North Bourn Pond Shelter
bourn-pond-shelter-north.notes: NBP Tenting area replaced shelter until 2011 GB

bourn-pond-shelter-south.name: South Bourn Pond Shelter
bourn-pond-shelter-south.notes: Reconstructed from  log fishing camps by Putney Summer Work Camp

camp-4.name: Camp 4
camp-4.notes: See: UVM long trail digital archives:Mountaintop logging camp and Camels Hump from lumber camp, this is likely mill two miles below camp #4

camp-webster-2.name: Camp Webster 2nd
camp-webster-2.notes: Called Old Webster and Webster Shelter

cooper-lodge.name: Cooper Lodge
cooper-lodge.notes: Originally called the Stone Hut,see 1937 GB

deer-view-cabin.name: Deer View Cabin
deer-view-cabin.notes: Replaces OLD Deer View Cabin, also called a camp

deers-leap-lodge.name: Deer's Leap Lodge
deers-leap-lodge.notes: Main Structure still standing as Long Trail Inn

duncan-camp.name: Duncan Camp
duncan-camp.notes: At Charles Hampton Grant farm in Peru Marge Fish husband researching location.

east-shore-shelter-at-little-rock-pond.name: East Shore Shelter at Little Rock Pond
east-shore-shelter-at-little-rock-pond.notes: Moved south .2 miles and renamed Lula Tye, photo shown is in new location with new name

gates-camp.name: Gates Camp
gates-camp.notes: Same site as Patch Camp according to guidebook

gifford-woods-state-park-shelters.name: Gifford Woods State Park Shelters
gifford-woods-state-park-shelters.notes: GF Park is mentioned in 1940 guidebook, the first shelters were built in 1939

glastenbury-mountain-forestry-cabin.name: Glastenbury Mountain Forestry Cabin
glastenbury-mountain-forestry-cabin.notes:
See: Glastenbury Mountain Cairn Site also 1930 GB
by Norman E Muller

goshen-lodge.name: Goshen Lodge
goshen-lodge.notes: There is something wrong with the description of this lodge on the web site.  It implies the shelter was on Camels Hump but in fact was near the new Sunrise Shelter (see Sunrise Shelters).  This is actually the first sunrise shelter, and then later part of the Sunrise Shelters. but early on called Goshen Lodge.

kiln-camp.name: Kiln Camp
kiln-camp.notes: Photo of Kilns in Griffith Village (Old Job) are not this location

lances-lodge.name: Lances Lodge
lances-lodge.notes: Shown on Section 10 maps for many years. Robert Lance Trail once connected mountain to Lodge.

little-rock-pond-camp.notes: I don't see mention of this camp in the 1928 GB

little-rock-pond-shelter-2.name: Little Rock Pond Shelter 2nd - B
little-rock-pond-shelter-2.notes: Was  Shelter#2 and dismantled and moved .1 mile north of pond in 1971

little-rock-pond-group-tenting-area.name: Little Rock Pond Group Tenting Area

luces-lookout.name: Luce's Lookout
luces-lookout.notes: Lookout view mentioned prior  to 1983 but not structures

mad-tom-shelter.name: Mad Tom Shelter
mad-tom-shelter.notes: Moved in 1980 and converted to a camp until 1997, Final location on Bromley at: 43.239637592610535, -72.93837765971196

montclair-glen-lodge-2.name: Montclair Glen Lodge 2nd
montclair-glen-lodge-2.notes: I think this is still standing as the current lodge

mount-mansfield-hotel.name: Mount Mansfield Hotel
mount-mansfield-hotel.notes: Torn down in 1964, Still mentioned in GB

patch-camp.name: Patch Camp
patch-camp.notes: Same structure as Gates Camp according to 1924 guidebook

rabbits-nest.name: Rabbit's Nest
rabbits-nest.notes: Are you sure you want to keep publishing this?

randall-lumber-camp.name: Randall Lumber Camp
randall-lumber-camp.notes: Not sure this is correct. The photo shown is actually the same photo of site #71 in Little River State Park book "the Beauiful Vale Above the Falls" pg.26,67. Although  1st GB shows trail ascending Gleason Brook to the summit in the area as descrribed so it just may be that this is not the correct  picture.

ritterbush-lodge.name: Ritterbush Lodge
ritterbush-lodge.notes: GB says this held 24, website says 8

seth-warner-shelter-1.name: Seth Warner Shelter 1st
seth-warner-shelter-1.notes: Built by carpenter trainees of the Manpower Development Training Act

shattuck-lodge.name: Shattuck Lodge
shattuck-lodge.architecture: Originally a logging camp and called Shattuck Lodge.  Then converted to Mould Lodge

stratton-mountain-vermont-forestry-servi.name: Stratton Mountain Vermont Forestry Service Cabin 1st
stratton-mountain-vermont-forestry-servi.notes: At spring below summit. Also called Porky lodge!

stratton-mountain-vermont-forestry-servi.name: Stratton Mountain Vermont Forestry Service Cabin 2nd
Stratton Mountain Vermont Forestry Service Cabin 3rd

sunnyside-camp-1.name: Sunnyside Camp 1st
sunnyside-camp-1.architecture: See Spring Lake Camp, was that shelter but moved to this location

sunrise-shelter-1.name: Sunrise Shelter 1st
sunrise-shelter-1.notes: Also called Sunrise Camps or Goshen Lodge, received a steel roof and abutting steel shelter in 1925

tillotson-camp-1.name: Tillotson Camp 1st
tillotson-camp-1.notes: Article states that this was one of the original shelters: https://www.northstarmonthly.com/columns/belvedere-mountain-and-the-great-white-hill/article_0e7df6a0-cd6b-11ed-86fd-b7fa99870fa8.html

wheeler-pond-camps.name: Wheeler Pond Camps
wheeler-pond-camps.notes: This was managed by the AMC prior to donation to the GMC.
